module_role: Module 4 of v17 integration — per-faction unit roster, mutation operations (Muster, commit, apply losses), and JSONL serialization. Foundational module with no v17 dependencies. Module 3 (Mass Battle Resolution) will consume this.

entries.section: §B.2 BG UNIT STATS (pre-printed on unit tokens)
entries.quoted_text: Levy | Light Infantry | Heavy Infantry | Cavalry | Archer | Crossbow | Sling | Artillery | Knights Templar — 9 BG unit classes per the §B.2 table

entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Unit-token state in Workstream A
entries.quoted_text: Active classes: 'Levy', 'LightInf', 'HeavyInf'

entries.quoted_text: Reserved: 'Cavalry', 'Archer', 'Crossbow', 'Sling', 'Artillery', 'KnightsTemplar'

entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Unit-token state
entries.quoted_text: Muster action: mints Levy tokens in territory (expandable to per-faction recruitment doctrine later).

entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — GAP: unit movement model
entries.quoted_text: (b) Commit from adjacent-to-target only (closer to canon; adds March prerequisite). Recommend (b) for canon fidelity, with March as implicit
entries.assumption: M4_ASSUMPTION_ONE: option (b) adopted as default per integration_plan recommendation. Canon does not finalize; M4 exposes constant for future override.

entries.value: defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int)) — outer key territory_id, inner key unit_class, value count
entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Unit-token state in Workstream A
entries.quoted_text: self.units = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int))

entries.value: {k: dict(v) for k, v in faction.units.items()}
entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Serialization note
entries.quoted_text: Serialization: json.dumps({k: dict(v) for k, v in faction.units.items()}) — defaultdict doesn't serialize directly.

entries.section: §B.3 BG Battle Resolution Step 3 — Roll
entries.quoted_text: Pool = sum of all engaged unit Martial values + commander bonus. Commander bonus = floor(faction Military / 2). [PP-555] TN 7. Ob from table.
entries.note: M4 supplies the unit-Martial-sum component; the commander bonus and TN are M3 responsibility.

entries.value: Pre-printed on Artillery and KnightsTemplar (HeavyBlunt units)
entries.section: §B.2 — Anti-Armour keyword
entries.quoted_text: Anti-Armour keyword (HeavyBlunt units): +2D when targeting Heavy Infantry, Cavalry, or Knights Templar. Pre-printed on Artillery and Knights Templar tokens.

entries.value: Pre-printed on Ranged (Archer, Crossbow, Sling) and Artillery
entries.section: §B.2 — Volley keyword
entries.quoted_text: Volley keyword (Ranged, Artillery): fires in Priority 1 before melee. Ranged cannot attack in melee same turn they Volley.

entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Muster action
entries.quoted_text: Muster action: mints Levy tokens in territory (expandable to per-faction recruitment doctrine later).
entries.assumption: M4_ASSUMPTION_THREE: single-territory Muster only; multi-territory recruitment, doctrine variation, and Levy Restriction (§A.14c) out of scope for M4.

entries.value: Adjacency required; active class only; sufficient count required
entries.section: §5 Phase 2c — Conquest action
entries.quoted_text: Conquest action: commits tokens from territory to battle.
entries.assumption: M4_ASSUMPTION_TWO: only active classes commitable. Reserved classes raise ValueError.

entries.value: Aggregate count reduction; no per-token identity
entries.section: §B.3 Step 5 — Apply damage
entries.quoted_text: Apply damage. Reduce Health per Step 3 net successes × unit damage modifier − DR. Formation Break at Health 0.
entries.assumption: M4_ASSUMPTION_FIVE: aggregate-count semantics. A loss of N units reduces the count; per-unit token identity not tracked at M4 layer.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Unit movement model — adjacency requirement for commit-to-battle
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon does not finalize the movement model. Integration plan recommends option (b) for canon fidelity. M4 implements (b) and exposes the constant for future override by M6 or M7.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: Jordan decision required to ratify or change. If movement model changes, only the COMMITMENT_ADJACENCY_MODEL constant + commit_to_battle adjacency check need to change.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Active vs reserved class semantics enforcement
provisional_assumptions.decision: All 9 classes are schema-complete in UNIT_STATS. Muster and commit_to_battle validate against ACTIVE_UNIT_CLASSES (3 classes). Reserved-class Muster or Commit raises ValueError.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Integration plan §5 Phase 2c specifies schema-complete + semantics-partial. M4 enforces the partial-semantics constraint with explicit ValueError rather than silent no-op so integration bugs surface immediately.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: When future modules activate reserved classes (e.g. Cavalry recruitment via Knights doctrine), they expand ACTIVE_UNIT_CLASSES. The validation gate then permits those classes automatically.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Muster scope — single territory, default Levy
provisional_assumptions.decision: Muster operates on a single territory at a time. Default class is Levy per integration_plan. Multi-territory recruitment, per-faction doctrine variation, and Levy Restriction (mass_battle §A.14c) are out of scope.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Integration plan explicitly defers these: 'expandable to per-faction recruitment doctrine later'. M4 stays narrow.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M6 (Faction Action Expansion) is the natural home for doctrine-specific recruitment overrides.

provisional_assumptions.decision: UnitRoster is per-faction state. Each faction owns its own roster instance. M4 does not coordinate cross-faction roster access.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Integration plan §5 Phase 2c codes it as 'self.units' on a Faction-equivalent object. M7 wires UnitRoster onto Faction objects.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 integration must attach a UnitRoster to each Faction at construction time.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Battle losses operate on aggregate counts, not per-unit tokens
provisional_assumptions.decision: apply_battle_losses takes {unit_class: count_to_remove} and reduces aggregate counts. There is no per-unit token identity at the M4 layer.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Integration plan and §B.3 Step 5 describe damage as a count-reducing operation (Formation Break at Health 0 is a per-class threshold, not a per-token state). Tracking per-unit identity would require a richer model not specified by canon.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: If named-unit-officers (§D.2 NPC named officers) becomes mechanically significant, that's a separate state layer (likely in a future module) that overlays per-faction unit rosters.
